data.title: EU regulator reportedly plans to launch probe into Meta over use of AI in WhatsApp; company calls claims 'baseless'
data.description:
The European Commission is planning to launch a new antitrust probe into Meta Platforms (META) over the rollout of AI features in WhatsApp, the Financial Times reported.

The EU's antitrust regulator was set to open the investigation into how the U.S. tech giant integrated its Meta AI system into the messaging service earlier this year, the report added, citing two officials.

Meta AI chatbot service has been built into WhatsApp's interface since March 2025 in European markets.

The European Commission did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

"The claims are baseless. The emergence of AI chatbots on our Business API puts a strain on our systems that they were not designed to support. Even still, the AI space is highly competitive and people have access to the services of their choice in any number of ways, including app stores, search engines, email services, partnership integrations, and operating systems," said a WhatsApp spokesperson in an email to Seeking Alpha.

The company told Reuters it has not received details of the investigation and pointed to an earlier WhatsApp statement on the Italian inquiry, which it dismissed as “unfounded.”

In July, the Italian Competition Authority said it launched an investigation into Meta, alleging that the company abused its dominant position by installing its AI service on the messaging app WhatsApp.

The investigation was expanded in November to examine if Meta further abused its dominance by blocking rival AI chatbots from the messaging platform, Reuters reported.

The European Commission is expected to announce the probe in the coming days, but the timing could change, FT reported, adding that the investigation will be conducted under traditional antitrust rules rather than the EU’s Digital Markets Act, the bloc's rules to govern several tech services from big tech companies.

data.title: Stock index futures muted as rate-cut talks stay in focus ahead of next week’s FOMC
data.description:
Stock index futures were muted on Thursday, as talks on rate cuts continued to be in focus ahead of next week's FOMC meeting.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) were flat, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.1% and Dow futures (INDU) +0.1%.

President Donald Trump's aides were reportedly in talks about appointing Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent as the top White House economic adviser, in addition to his current role, if Kevin Hassett is picked as the next Federal Reserve chair.

On the economic side, the initial jobless claims and the international trade in goods and services reports will land before the bell. Factory orders and the Fed balance sheet will come in later in the day. 

"This year’s U.S. labor story has been of companies reluctant to hire, so initial claims may not capture all the issues (new entrants to the labor market may not be claiming benefits). The 'no hire' story does not seem to be AI-related (although AI is a convenient cover story to excuse the CEO’s past bad decisions)," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

data.title: China’s Cambricon aims to triple chip output to fill Nvidia void - Bloomberg
data.description:
Chinese chipmaker Cambricon Technologies plans to triple AI chip production in 2026, aiming to capture market share from Huawei Technologies and fill the gap left by Nvidia’s (NVDA) forced exit from China, Bloomberg reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

Founded in 2016, Cambricon offers a series of intelligent chip products and platform-based basic system software. The Beijing-based company is preparing to deliver half a million artificial intelligence accelerators in 2026, the people said, including up to 300,000 units of its most advanced Siyuan 590 and 690 chips, built mainly on SMIC’s (OTCQX:SIUIF) 7nm “N+2” production process.

The expansion reflects China’s push to replace U.S. technology, with Huawei also doubling output over the next year and Moore Threads entering the market. Earlier this year, China's regulators reportedly summoned local chipmakers to report how their products compare against Nvidia’s China chips and concluded that China’s AI processors have reached a level equal to or exceeding that of the Nvidia chips allowed under export controls.

Nvidia’s (NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang in November acknowledged the company is effectively shut out of China, opening space for domestic rivals.

Cambricon’s success, however, will hinge on securing SMIC capacity at a time other rivals are also vying to place orders with China’s most advanced chipmaker. For context, the company will build just 142,000 AI chips in 2025, per Goldman Sachs.

SMIC’s own technology may prove an obstacle. Yields for Cambricon’s top-of-the-line 590 and 690 chips are currently only about 20%, the people said, meaning roughly four out of five silicon dies are considered flawed. By comparison, leading foundry TSMC (TSM) is estimated to achieve 60%+ yields on its advanced 2nm process — a node roughly three generations (seven years) ahead of SMIC, analysts say.

The supply of the high-bandwidth memory chips required to make AI accelerators may be another potential bottleneck.

data.title: European markets extend gains following global cues
data.description:
London (UKX) -0.07% to 9,689, marking a fourth straight day in the red. New passenger car registrations dropped 1.60% in November from 0.50% in October of 2025. 

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.88% at 23,902, HCOB Germany Construction PMI rose to 45.2 in November

France (CAC:IND) +0.45% to 8,123, HCOB Construction PMI in France rose to 43.6 in November.

The HCOB Eurozone Construction PMI rose to 45.4 in November.

In other parts of Europe, the Austrian economy expanded by 0.4% Q/Q in Q3.

The average monthly wages in the Czech Republic rose by 4.5% Y/Y in Q3.

Switzerland’s non-seasonally adjusted unemployment rate was unchanged at 2.9% in November 2025.

Retail sales in Hungary increased by 3.1% Y/Y in October.

Sweden posted a current account surplus of SEK 93.2 billion in Q3. Sweden’s annual inflation rate eased to 0.3% in November.

HCOB Italy Construction PMI fell to 48.2 in November.

Retail sales in Slovakia rose by 0.6% Y/Y in October.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) traded 0.34% higher to 578.2, extending gains from the previous two sessions and reflecting a more positive tone across global markets. Traders are monitoring developments in the Ukraine peace talks.

Coming up in the session: Euro Area retail sales expected shortly

In the bond market, the yield on the US 10-year Treasury was up 3 basis points to 4.09%.

UK's 10-year yield was down less than 1 basis point to 4.46%. 

Germany's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point to 2.76%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Trump aides discuss tapping Bessent to lead National Economic Council - report
data.description:
President Trump's aides are discussing appointing Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent as the top White House economic adviser, in addition to his current role, if Kevin Hassett is picked as the next Federal Reserve chair, people familiar with the matter told Bloomberg News.

Hassett, who is the director of the National Economic Council, is widely viewed as the frontrunner to take up the top job at the Fed. Trump said he'd announce his nominee for the role early next year.

The potential moves are not finalized. Any personnel changes should be considered speculation until announced by the president, a White House official told Bloomberg.

Bessent already serves as the acting Internal Revenue Service commissioner, in addition to being the Treasury secretary. If he is also named as the NEC director, he would become the chief arbiter of the Trump administration's economic portfolio.

The NEC's role is to advise the president on U.S. and international economic policy, and coordinate policymaking decisions across the federal government.

But the NEC has played a diminished role in the current Trump administration, and Hassett has largely served as an advocate for Trump's policies.

The NEC director job does not require Senate approval. Trump's pick for Fed chair would require Senate confirmation.

data.title: Novo Nordisk pushes ahead on Alzheimer’s efforts; said to plan December Ozempic rollout in India
data.description:
Danish drugmaker Novo Nordisk (NVO) has reportedly left open the door for additional work on its pill version of Ozempic for Alzheimer’s disease after a pair of failed trials, saying that patients showed a biological response in a handful of areas despite getting no cognitive improvement.

Doctors found reductions of 10% or less in a handful of markers of neurodegeneration, inflammation, and tau, an abnormal protein deposit linked to Alzheimer’s disease, based on measurements of patients’ cerebrospinal fluid, Bloomberg News reported, citing a company presentation at the Clinical Trials on Alzheimer’s Disease conference on Wednesday in San Diego. 

Elsewhere, Reuters reported that Novo Nordisk (NVO) will launch its blockbuster diabetes drug Ozempic in India this month as it seeks to strengthen its foothold in the world's most populous nation.

Ozempic, a once-weekly injectable approved by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration in 2017 for type 2 diabetes, has become a global bestseller and is widely used off-label for weight loss due to its appetite-suppressing effects.

Novo's Wegovy, which like Ozempic is semaglutide, is approved for weight loss.

"Novo wants Ozempic in the market before generics bring out cheaper versions," one of the sources said, adding the launch could happen within the next three to four weeks.

Novo, which in September said it secured Indian regulatory approval to import and sell Ozempic, on Thursday said it hoped to launch the drug as early as possible, but did not give further details on an updated timeline or the drug's pricing, the report added.

The company's shares are down 44% YTD.

data.title: Exxon prepares to wind down one of two steam crackers in Singapore - report
data.description:
ExxonMobil (XOM) plans to permanently shut down its older steam cracker on Jurong Island, Singapore, in March 2026, Reuters reported, citing sources familiar with the matter.

The shutdown process is expected to be completed by June 2026, the sources added. The facility, which has been operational since 2002, faces closure amid global petrochemical overcapacity driven by Chinese production.

The move aligns with broader industry-wide losses, workforce reduction in Singapore, and sales of retail operations. 

Earlier this year, the company started a 1.6 million tons/year ethylene cracker in Huizhou, China, and also gradually reduced term contract volumes with customers in Singapore. The company will continue operating a second, newer cracker, started in 2013 on the island.

ExxonMobil (XOM) did not immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

data.title: United Microelectronics' November revenue rose ~6% Y/Y
data.description:
• United Microelectronics (UMC) reported revenue of NT$21.23B for November 2025, marking a growth of 5.91% year-on-year.
  • For Jan.-Nov. 2025, revenue totaled NT$218.27B, up 2.31% Y/Y.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: UP Fintech Non-GAAP EPADS of $0.31, touches record revenue of $175.2M
data.description:
• UP Fintech press release (TIGR): Q3 Non-GAAP EPADS of $0.31.
  • Revenue of $175.2M (+73.3% Y/Y)
  • GAAP EPADS of $0.27
  • Non-GAAP net income attributable to UP Fintech shareholders surged to US$57.0 million, up 28.2% QoQ and 2.8 times the level of the same period last year, also a record high.
  • Commissions were US$72.9 million, an increase of 76.9% from US$41.2 million in the same quarter of last year, due to an increase in trading volume.
  • Funded accounts increased by 31,500, bringing total funded clients up 18.5% YoY to 1.22 million.
  • Market activity remained robust, with Q3 trading volume climbing 28.5% YoY to US$209.4 billion.
  • Total client assets hit a new peak of US$61.0 billion, rising 17.3% sequentially and 49.7% YoY. 
  • "Tiger Vault" grew 339% YoY in AUC in Hong Kong
